summary: Selected recent papers emphasize light-ion bottomonium suppression, D-meson diffusion at compressed baryonic densities, heteroscedastic nuclear-model uncertainty, neural-accelerated dense-matter calibration, and cautionary AI/ML workflows where automation or density estimation outpaces validation evidence.
strongest_paper: 2607.13758: CMS evidence for sequential Upsilon(nS) suppression in light-ion collisions, directly relevant to QGP formation in small collision systems.

papers.title: Evidence for sequential Upsilon(nS) suppression in light ion collisions
papers.abstract: CMS reports Upsilon(1S), Upsilon(2S), and Upsilon(3S) measurements in O+O collisions and Upsilon(1S), Upsilon(2S) in Ne+Ne at sqrt(sNN)=5.36 TeV, finding suppressed excited-to-ground-state ratios relative to pp and evidence for sequential suppression in light-ion collisions.

papers.title: Towards compressed baryonic matter densities: D meson diffusion
papers.authors: Dani Rose J Marattukalam, Manpreet Kaur, Arvind Kumar, Sabyasachi Ghosh
papers.abstract: The paper computes D-meson spatial diffusion and momentum-transport coefficients in dense nuclear matter using relaxation-time kinetic theory with a chiral SU(3) hadronic model, comparing dilute and degenerate gas approximations across density regimes.

papers.title: Beyond Constant Error: Heteroscedastic Bayesian Model Combination for Modeling Unmeasured Nuclei
papers.authors: B. Knight, S. Lalit, P. Giuliani, K. Godbey, W. Nazarewicz, A. Ravlic, P.-G. Reinhard
papers.abstract: The paper extends Bayesian Model Combination so the ensemble uncertainty is heteroscedastic and can grow in extrapolative regions, validating the method with experimental and synthetic data for energy-density-functional predictions of unmeasured nuclei.

papers.title: Neural-Accelerated Bayesian Calibration of Chiral Mean-Field Models to Nuclear Saturation and Vacuum Properties
papers.authors: Isaac Legred, Mateus Reinke Pelicer, Veronica Dexheimer, Jacquelyn Noronha-Hostler, Nicolas Yunes
papers.abstract: The paper uses a neural-network surrogate inside a Bayesian calibration workflow for chiral mean-field models, interfacing MUSES, Bilby, and PyTorch and highlighting degeneracies among viable dense-matter parameter sets.

papers.title: NNStar: An end-to-end AI agent for nuclear matter and neutron star physics
papers.authors: Yao Ma, Yong-Liang Ma, Jia-Ying Xiong
papers.abstract: The paper presents an LLM-agent skill that automates relativistic mean-field model construction, EoS generation, TOV integration, and Bayesian scoring for nuclear matter and neutron-star observables.

papers.title: Mono-Z Dark Matter Search with Neural Spline Flows Using CMS Run 2015D Open Data
papers.authors: Hitesh Rasineni, Bhavishya Chebrolu et al.
papers.abstract: The paper trains neural spline flows for signal/background density estimation and likelihood-ratio scoring in a CMS open-data mono-Z dark-matter search, but reports observed limits weakened by a residual high-MET background-modeling discrepancy.
